evals.prompt: Set up my status page context
evals.expected_output: Should check for existing .agents/status-page-context.md, find none, offer auto-draft or start-from-scratch modes, then walk through all sections of the template.
assertions: Checks for .agents/status-page-context.md
Offers auto-draft (scan codebase) and start-from-scratch options
Covers all sections: Service Overview, Components, SLA, Tone, Severity, Escalation, Channels, Past Patterns
Writes output to .agents/status-page-context.md

evals.prompt: Auto-generate my status page context from the codebase
evals.expected_output: Should scan README, config files, package.json, and any status page configuration to pre-fill as much as possible, then ask the user to confirm and fill gaps.
assertions: Reads README and config files for product info
Pre-fills company name and product description
Identifies components from codebase if possible
Clearly marks sections that need user input
Asks user to review and complete missing sections

evals.prompt: Update my status page context — we added a new Webhooks component
evals.expected_output: Should read existing .agents/status-page-context.md, add the new component to the components table, and write the updated file.
assertions: Reads existing .agents/status-page-context.md
Adds new component to the components table
Asks for description and criticality of the new component
Preserves all other existing content
Writes updated file back to .agents/status-page-context.md

evals.prompt: Our incident updates should sound like a calm senior engineer explaining to a peer, not a PR statement
evals.expected_output: Should update the Communication Tone section with specific voice characteristics, example sentences, and words to use/avoid that match this style.
assertions: Updates Communication Tone section specifically
Captures the 'senior engineer to peer' voice
Includes words to avoid (corporate jargon, deflections)
Includes words to use (direct, technical terms)
Provides at least one example sentence in the described tone

evals.prompt: I just want to set the company name and skip everything else
evals.expected_output: Should allow setting the company name but warn that incomplete context will reduce the quality of other skills' output. Should not block the user but clearly flag what's missing.
assertions: Allows partial completion without blocking
Warns about impact on downstream skills
Lists which sections are incomplete
Offers to come back and fill in sections later
Still writes the file with completed sections

evals.prompt: Write me an incident update for our API outage
evals.expected_output: Should redirect the user to the incident-communication skill instead of attempting to write an incident update.
assertions: Does not write an incident update
Points user to the incident-communication skill
Offers to set up or update status page context if needed
